response: <<Transform the>> <<conditional structure>> <<in the>> <<form of>> <<an if>> <<statement to>> <<an equivalent>> <<switch control>> <<flow variant>>. <<Here is>> <<the if>> <<statement if>> <<grade is>> <<A then>> <<gradeValue becomes>> <<4.0 else>> <<if grade>> <<is B>> <<gradeValue becomes>> <<3.0 else>> <<if grade>> <<is C>> <<gradeValue becomes>> <<2.0 else>> <<if grade>> <<is D>> <<gradeValue becomes>> <<1.0 Your>> <<task is>> <<not only>> <<to interchange>> <<if with>> <<a switch>> <<scenario but>> <<also to>> <<ensure that>> <<the resulting>> <<function has>> <<optimal efficiency>>. <<Wrap every>> <<word bigram>> <<in double>> <<angular brackets>> <<such as>> <<I am>> <<at home>> <<with my>> <<cute dog>>. <<switch grade>> <<case A>> <<gradeValue equals>> <<4.0 break>> <<case B>> <<gradeValue equals>> <<3.0 break>> <<case C>> <<gradeValue equals>> <<2.0 break>> <<case D>> <<gradeValue equals>> <<1.0 break>> <<default do>> <<nothing or>> <<throw error>>. <<This switch>> <<version avoids>> <<multiple comparisons>> <<by using>> <<direct jumps>> <<for better>> <<performance when>> <<grades are>> <<known constants>>. <<Efficiency gains>> <<come from>> <<hashing or>> <<jump tables>> <<instead of>> <<sequential checks>>. <<Thus the>> <<switch block>> <<replaces the>> <<if chain>> <<effectively and>> <<optimally for>> <<this specific>> <<use case>>.
